examples.title: Recursive Factorial Function
examples.description: Computes factorial of a number using recursion
examples.explanation: The function checks for base case (n=0 or 1 returns 1) then multiplies n with factorial of n-1. Each call adds to stack until base case is reached, then returns propagate back multiplying all values.

examples.title: Binary Search Using Recursion
examples.description: Searches for an element in a sorted array using divide and conquer
examples.explanation: The array is divided in half at each step. If key equals mid, found. If key is smaller, search left half; otherwise search right half. Recursion continues until element is found or search space is exhausted.

examples.title: Tower of Hanoi
examples.description: Solves Tower of Hanoi puzzle using recursion
examples.explanation: For n disks: first move n-1 disks to auxiliary peg, then move largest disk to destination, then move n-1 disks from auxiliary to destination. This creates 2^n - 1 total moves.

examples.title: Fibonacci with Memoization
examples.explanation: Memoization stores computed Fibonacci values in an array. When fibonacci(n) is called, it first checks if the value is already in memo array. If yes, returns it immediately without recalculation, avoiding exponential time.

examples.title: Reverse String Using Recursion
examples.description: Reverses a string in place using recursion
examples.explanation: The function takes two indices (start and end). It swaps characters at these positions, then recursively calls itself with incremented start and decremented end. Base case triggers when indices meet or cross.
